subject: Social Sciences
title: Democracy Game
tagline: Relive the 1994 elections and the birth of South African democracy!

stages.name: Stage 1
stages.question: What does 'democracy' mean?
options: Freedom of speech
A public vote on a specific issue
Government by the people
By voting in elections
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Government by the people is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Government by the people.

stages.name: Stage 2
stages.question: How do citizens choose their leaders in a democracy?
options: By voting in elections
Citizens 18 and older
A public vote on a specific issue
The supreme law of a country
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! By voting in elections is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is By voting in elections.

stages.name: Stage 3
stages.question: What is a 'constitution'?
options: The option with the most votes wins
They let citizens choose leaders fairly
The supreme law of a country
A public vote on a specific issue
stages.correctOption: The supreme law of a country
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The supreme law of a country is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The supreme law of a country.

stages.name: Stage 4
stages.question: Why is it important to have more than one political party?
options: The supreme law of a country
It gives citizens real choices
Citizens 18 and older
A public vote on a specific issue
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! It gives citizens real choices is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is It gives citizens real choices.

stages.name: Stage 5
stages.question: What is a 'referendum'?
options: By voting in elections
The supreme law of a country
Freedom of speech
A public vote on a specific issue
stages.correctOption: A public vote on a specific issue
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A public vote on a specific issue is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A public vote on a specific issue.

stages.name: Stage 6
stages.question: What does 'majority rule' mean?
options: By voting in elections
Government by the people
They let citizens choose leaders fairly
The option with the most votes wins
stages.correctOption: The option with the most votes wins
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The option with the most votes wins is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The option with the most votes wins.

stages.name: Stage 7
stages.question: Why are free elections important?
options: Government by the people
They let citizens choose leaders fairly
It gives citizens real choices
Citizens 18 and older
stages.correctOption: They let citizens choose leaders fairly
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! They let citizens choose leaders fairly is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is They let citizens choose leaders fairly.

stages.name: Stage 8
stages.question: What is a 'political party'?
options: A group with shared political goals
Citizens 18 and older
Freedom of speech
The supreme law of a country
stages.correctOption: A group with shared political goals
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A group with shared political goals is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A group with shared political goals.

stages.name: Stage 9
stages.question: What right allows citizens to share their opinions publicly?
options: Freedom of speech
It gives citizens real choices
Government by the people
A group with shared political goals
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Freedom of speech is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Freedom of speech.

stages.name: Stage 10
stages.question: Who can vote in a South African national election?
options: The supreme law of a country
A group with shared political goals
The option with the most votes wins
Citizens 18 and older
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Citizens 18 and older is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Citizens 18 and older.
